symbols.name: n
symbols.def: sample size in the Gaussian singular-coordinate experiment

symbols.name: g^R
symbols.def: orthogonal projection of g onto closure(range(T))

symbols.name: h^Pi
symbols.def: minimum-norm element satisfying T h^Pi = g^R

symbols.name: (sigma_j, u_j, v_j)_{j>=1}
symbols.def: singular system of T with T v_j = sigma_j u_j and T^* u_j = sigma_j v_j

symbols.name: u_j
symbols.def: jth left singular function of T

symbols.name: v_j
symbols.def: jth right singular function of T

symbols.name: psi
symbols.def: fixed loading sequence defining the target linear functional

symbols.name: b
symbols.def: benchmark sequence governing the decay profile of psi

symbols.name: Z_{j,n}
symbols.def: jth Gaussian sequence observation at sample size n

symbols.name: tau
symbols.def: decay exponent of the functional representer profile

assumptions.condition: T admits the singular system (sigma_j, u_j, v_j)_{j>=1} with sigma_j decreasing to 0.

standard.name: least-squares domain condition for the no-solution target

assumptions.condition: For each j >= 1, Z_{j,n} = sigma_j theta_j^Pi + n^(-1/2) xi_j with (xi_j)_{j>=1} independent N(0,1).

standard.name: continuity of the linear functional on the source class

assumptions.condition: psi belongs to Psi_asymp(b, c_{b,-}, c_{b,+}).
There exist positive constants c_sigma_minus and c_sigma_plus such that c_sigma_minus j^(-alpha) <= sigma_j <= c_sigma_plus j^(-alpha) for all j >= 1.

standard.name: mild ill-posedness with polynomial singular-value decay

assumptions.condition: There exist positive constants c_a_minus and c_a_plus such that c_a_minus j^(-beta) <= a_j <= c_a_plus j^(-beta) for all j >= 1.
There exist positive constants c_btau_minus and c_btau_plus such that c_btau_minus j^(-tau) <= b_j <= c_btau_plus j^(-tau) for all j >= 1.

standard.name: polynomial continuity region for the linear functional

assumptions.condition: There exist positive constants c_exp_minus and c_exp_plus such that c_exp_minus exp(-alpha j) <= sigma_j <= c_exp_plus exp(-alpha j) for all j >= 1.

standard.name: severe ill-posedness with exponential singular-value decay

assumptions.condition: There exist positive constants c_aexp_minus and c_aexp_plus such that c_aexp_minus exp(-beta j) <= a_j <= c_aexp_plus exp(-beta j) for all j >= 1.
There exist positive constants c_bexp_minus and c_bexp_plus such that c_bexp_minus exp(-tau j) <= b_j <= c_bexp_plus exp(-tau j) for all j >= 1.

definitions.name: Psi_cont(a)
definitions.construction: { psi = (psi_j)_{j>=1} in l2(N) : sum_{j>=1} a_j^2 psi_j^2 < infinity }

definitions.name: Psi_asymp(b, c_{b,-}, c_{b,+})
definitions.construction: { psi = (psi_j)_{j>=1} in l2(N) : c_{b,-} b_j <= |psi_j| <= c_{b,+} b_j for all j >= 1 }

definitions.name: G_n(a,L)
definitions.construction: { P_n : under P_n at sample size n, ass:projection-domain, ass:gaussian-sequence, and ass:projected-source hold }

definitions.name: hat_theta_{psi,lambda}^{(k)}
definitions.construction: hat_theta_{psi,lambda}^{(k)} = sum_{j>=1} psi_j q_{k,lambda}(sigma_j^2) sigma_j Z_{j,n}

definitions.name: R_{n,k}^{it}(psi)
definitions.construction: R_{n,k}^{it}(psi) = inf_{lambda>0}{ n^(-1/2) [sum_{j>=1} psi_j^2 sigma_j^2 q_{k,lambda}(sigma_j^2)^2]^(1/2) + L [sum_{j>=1} a_j^2 psi_j^2 (1 - sigma_j^2 q_{k,lambda}(sigma_j^2))^2]^(1/2) }

statements.statement: Assume ass:singular-system, ass:projection-domain, ass:gaussian-sequence, ass:projected-source, and ass:functional-continuity-membership. For every fixed qualification order k >= 1 there exists C_k > 0 such that any lambda_n^star attaining def:iterated-risk-frontier within a constant factor satisfies sup_{P in G_n(a,L)} E_P[|hat_theta_{psi,lambda_n^star}^{(k)} - theta_psi|] <= C_k R_{n,k}^{it}(psi). There also exists C_cut > 0 such that any m_n^star attaining def:cutoff-frontier within a constant factor satisfies sup_{P in G_n(a,L)} E_P[|hat_theta_{psi,m_n^star}^{cut} - theta_psi|] <= C_cut R_n^{cut}(psi).
statements.justification: This theorem exhibits the higher-qualification estimators that a valid Tikhonov-suboptimality claim must beat first-order Tikhonov with.
statements.gap: ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV gives first-order Tikhonov achievability for the no-solution target, while BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023SourceConditionDR studies iterated Tikhonov for generic inverse-problem functionals rather than this least-squares projection geometry.
statements.consumer: Supplies the feasible filter family needed for any later theorem that says the Shen baseline is a qualification ceiling rather than an intrinsic lower bound.

statements.statement: Assume thm:filter-upper's hypotheses together with ass:functional-profile-membership, ass:poly-spectral, ass:poly-source-profile, ass:poly-functional-profile, ass:poly-continuity, and ass:poly-irregular. If k > beta, then R_{n,k}^{it}(psi) and R_n^{cut}(psi) are both of order n^(-(beta + tau - 1 / 2) / (2 (alpha + beta))). By contrast, first-order Tikhonov satisfies R_{n,1}^{it}(psi) of order n^(-(min{beta, 1} + tau - 1 / 2) / (2 (alpha + min{beta, 1}))). Consequently, whenever beta > 1, sup_{P in G_n(a,L)} E_P[|hat_theta_{psi,lambda_n^star}^{(k)} - theta_psi|] = o(sup_{P in G_n(a,L)} E_P[|hat_theta_{psi,lambda_n^star}^{(1)} - theta_psi|]) for any k > beta and for the cutoff estimator.
statements.justification: This is the new regime theorem: the rate loss from first-order Tikhonov is pinned to its finite qualification, not to the no-solution target itself.
statements.gap: BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR derives exact-solution linear-functional rates, and LoGerfoRosascoOdoneDeVitoVerri2008SpectralAlgorithms explains qualification for spectral filters, but neither paper computes the no-solution projection-functional gap between first-order Tikhonov and higher-qualification filters.
statements.consumer: Tells an applied NPIV researcher exactly when the Shen baseline should be replaced by an iterated-Tikhonov or cutoff implementation in mildly ill-posed problems.

statements.statement: If g belongs to range(T) and h^Pi equals the exact NPIV structural function h_0 solving T h_0 = g, then thm:filter-upper and prop:poly-qualification-gap reduce to the exact-solution linear-functional filter comparisons of BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR and the adaptive exact-solution benchmark of BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR.
statements.justification: The proposal needs a solvable-case collapse to show the no-solution geometry is extending, not replacing, the classical NPIR benchmark.
statements.gap: The exact-solution formulas are already known from BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR; the new content is the reduction from the no-solution least-squares projection target back to that benchmark.
statements.consumer: Gives the referee and later formal stages a concrete reduction test for checking that the no-solution filter comparison has the right classical limit.

statements.statement: Assume thm:filter-upper's hypotheses together with ass:functional-profile-membership, ass:exp-spectral, ass:exp-source-profile, ass:exp-functional-profile, and ass:exp-irregular. Then there exists an explicit higher-qualification estimator, obtainable either as spectral cutoff or as iterated Tikhonov with k > beta, whose worst-case risk is of order n^(-(beta + tau) / (2 (alpha + beta))). The same rate is the intrinsic minimax modulus on the corresponding no-solution projection class, and first-order Tikhonov is intrinsically rate-suboptimal whenever beta > 1. On the polynomial class of prop:poly-qualification-gap, the same intrinsic-optimality conclusion also holds, so the displayed faster rate is not merely an algorithmic improvement over one regularizer.
statements.justification: This conjecture isolates the genuinely open part: turning the qualification-driven upper gap into an intrinsic minimax frontier, especially in the severely ill-posed regime.
statements.gap: ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV does not prove that first-order Tikhonov is intrinsically sharp, BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023SourceConditionDR does not treat the no-solution projection target, and no published paper appears to combine the no-solution target with a lower bound that certifies qualification-induced saturation.
statements.consumer: Would settle whether faster no-solution NPIV inference requires better filters only in practice or because the statistical problem itself allows faster rates than first-order Tikhonov delivers.

estimand_functional: R_{n,k}^{it}(psi) = inf_{lambda>0}{ n^(-1/2) [sum_{j>=1} psi_j^2 sigma_j^2 q_{k,lambda}(sigma_j^2)^2]^(1/2) + L [sum_{j>=1} a_j^2 psi_j^2 (1 - sigma_j^2 q_{k,lambda}(sigma_j^2))^2]^(1/2) }

sampling_model.design: Gaussian singular-coordinate reduction of the no-solution NPIV least-squares projection experiment
sampling_model.units: For each sample size n, observe the infinite Gaussian sequence (Z_{j,n})_{j>=1}
sampling_model.treatment: No binary treatment indicator enters the reduced experiment; the target is a finite-dimensional linear functional of h^Pi
sampling_model.overlap: Continuity is encoded by psi in Psi_cont(a) and, for rate comparisons, by psi in Psi_asymp(b, c_{b,-}, c_{b,+})
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: No nuisance learner is estimated inside the reduced experiment; the open embedding back to the i.i.d. NPIV model is explicitly deferred

bibliography.citation: Zikai Shen, Nathan Kallus, Dimitri Meunier, Houssam Zenati, Arthur Gretton, and Aurelien Bibaut (2026). Nonparametric Instrumental Variable Analysis Without Structural Equations: Debiased Inference on Functionals of Inverse Problems with No Solutions. arXiv:2604.24660.

Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Whitney Newey, Vasilis Syrgkanis, and Masatoshi Uehara (2023). Source Condition Double Robust Inference on Functionals of Inverse Problems. arXiv:2307.13793.
Christoph Breunig and Jan Johannes (2009). On Rate Optimal Local Estimation in Nonparametric Instrumental Regression. arXiv:0902.2103.
Christoph Breunig and Jan Johannes (2011). Adaptive Estimation of Functionals in Nonparametric Instrumental Regression. arXiv:1109.0961.
Xiaohong Chen and Markus Reiss (2011). On Rate Optimality for Ill-Posed Inverse Problems in Econometrics. Econometric Theory 27(3): 497-521.

Marine Carrasco, Jean-Pierre Florens, and Eric Renault (2007). Linear Inverse Problems in Structural Econometrics Estimation Based on Spectral Decomposition and Regularization. In Handbook of Econometrics, Volume 6B.
L. Lo Gerfo, Lorenzo Rosasco, Francesca Odone, Ernesto De Vito, and Alessandro Verri (2008). Spectral Algorithms for Supervised Learning. Neural Computation 20(7): 1873-1897.
Peter Hall and Joel L. Horowitz (2006). Nonparametric Methods for Inference in the Presence of Instrumental Variables. Annals of Statistics 33(6): 2904-2929.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: The no-solution NPIV projection target admits first-order Tikhonov upper-rate inference under projected source conditions.
Iterated Tikhonov regularized adversarial estimators can support inference for linear functionals of inverse problems under source conditions.
Exact-solution NPIR linear functionals admit rate-optimal spectral estimators under source and representer profiles.
Qualification of spectral filters determines how source smoothness is translated into bias decay.

tldr: The dead angle tried to characterize the intrinsic modulus directly. This pivot changes the focal object: it studies the qualification gap between first-order Tikhonov and explicit higher-qualification filters for the same no-solution NPIV least-squares projection functional. The theorem-level core is an oracle upper bound for iterated Tikhonov and spectral cutoff, followed by a polynomial regime theorem showing that first-order Tikhonov saturates once the projected-source smoothness exponent exceeds one, while higher-qualification filters keep converting extra smoothness into faster rates. The open content is whether that faster rate is also the intrinsic minimax modulus, especially under severe ill-posedness, so the proposal states that step honestly as a conjecture rather than smuggling it in as a theorem.

project_justification.gap: Shen et al. make the no-solution target operational but only through first-order Tikhonov upper rates. The literature on spectral qualification explains why first-order Tikhonov can saturate, yet no published paper appears to work out that comparison for least-squares projection functionals that remain well-defined when the NPIV structural equation has no exact solution.
project_justification.niche: The niche is narrower and more referee-defensible than the dead angle's full converse: compare explicit regularization schemes on the no-solution target under the same source and representer profiles, then separate algorithmic saturation from intrinsic hardness.
project_justification.fill: The project fills it by exhibiting higher-qualification filters inside the no-solution Gaussian sequence experiment, proving a mild-ill-posed qualification gap theorem, and isolating the genuinely open severe-ill-posed and minimax-optimality steps as one conjecture.

related_work: ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV is the baseline because it defines the no-solution least-squares projection target and proves first-order Tikhonov achievability, but it does not compare filter qualification on that target. BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023SourceConditionDR is the closest methodological neighbor because it shows how iterated Tikhonov can matter for inference on inverse-problem functionals, yet its parameter is a generic linear functional of a solvable inverse problem rather than a no-solution NPIV projection functional. BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR are the closest exact-solution functional comparators: they derive rate-optimal and adaptive spectral estimators under source and representer profiles, which is exactly the template that the no-solution reduction must recover in the solvable case. CarrascoFlorensRenault2007LinearInverseEconometrics provides the econometric regularization background for source conditions and filter comparisons, while LoGerfoRosascoOdoneDeVitoVerri2008SpectralAlgorithms makes the qualification mechanism explicit across spectral filters. ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics and HallHorowitz2006NonparametricMethodsIV remain relevant because they supply the rate algebra for mildly and severely ill-posed inverse problems, but they do not treat the least-squares projection target that survives the failure of the structural equation itself.
honest_scope: The proposal is intentionally not another direct minimax-converse claim. Theorem 1 and Proposition 1 live in the reduced Gaussian sequence experiment, where the regularization comparison is mathematically transparent. Proposition 1 is only stated for the polynomial non-root-n regime because that is the qualification calculation that is already structurally clear. The severe ill-posed classifier and the claim that the faster rate is intrinsic rather than merely algorithmic are the actual open content, so they appear in Conjecture 1 instead of being hidden inside a theorem. The two-sided representer profile restriction is narrower than an unrestricted ellipsoid class; that narrowing is deliberate because the angle is about filter saturation on a profile subclass, not about the entire realized-functional modulus problem that failed as the prior kernel.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: The exhausted angle 0 in this qid centered the realized-functional intrinsic modulus R_n^*(psi) and treated filter comparison only as support material. This pivot replaces that focal object with a different one: a qualification-gap theorem comparing first-order Tikhonov against explicit higher-qualification filters on the same no-solution target. In repo terms, the new angle is closer in shape to the older CausalSmith warnings about treating Tikhonov support material as a flagship object, but it avoids reusing the dead kernel's converse and instead asks for a direct estimator-comparison theorem plus one honest intrinsic-optimality conjecture.
novelty_justification.published_axis: The closest published paper is ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV, whose contribution is an achievability theory for the no-solution projection target using first-order Tikhonov. BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023SourceConditionDR is the closest higher-qualification inference paper, but it does not analyze the no-solution least-squares projection target. BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR give the exact-solution linear-functional rate template, and LoGerfoRosascoOdoneDeVitoVerri2008SpectralAlgorithms explains qualification abstractly, but no paper appears to combine those ingredients into an explicit no-solution NPIV theorem saying when first-order Tikhonov saturates and when an exhibited higher-qualification filter is strictly faster.

literature_checklist.author: Zikai Shen, Nathan Kallus, Dimitri Meunier, Houssam Zenati, Arthur Gretton, and Aurelien Bibaut
literature_checklist.one_line: Defines the no-solution NPIV least-squares projection target and establishes first-order Tikhonov upper-rate inference.

literature_checklist.author: Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Whitney Newey, Vasilis Syrgkanis, and Masatoshi Uehara
literature_checklist.one_line: Shows how iterated Tikhonov regularization can support functional inference in inverse problems when qualification matters.

literature_checklist.author: Christoph Breunig and Jan Johannes
literature_checklist.one_line: Provides the exact-solution NPIR linear-functional rate formulas under source and representer profiles.

literature_checklist.author: Christoph Breunig and Jan Johannes
literature_checklist.one_line: Develops adaptive exact-solution NPIR functional estimation, clarifying which parts of the new angle are already classical in the solvable case.

literature_checklist.author: Marine Carrasco, Jean-Pierre Florens, and Eric Renault
literature_checklist.one_line: Supplies the econometric spectral-regularization and source-condition framework used to formulate the qualification comparison.

literature_checklist.author: L. Lo Gerfo, Lorenzo Rosasco, Francesca Odone, Ernesto De Vito, and Alessandro Verri
literature_checklist.one_line: Makes explicit that finite qualification can cap the bias improvement delivered by Tikhonov-type filters.

literature_checklist.author: Xiaohong Chen and Markus Reiss
literature_checklist.one_line: Provides the Gaussian-sequence lower-bound and severe-ill-posed rate algebra that the conjectured intrinsic frontier would adapt.
